name: Kentucky Lake (KY)
description:
Kentucky Lake is one of the largest man-made lakes in the eastern United States, stretching 184 miles along the Tennessee River. The Kentucky portion of the lake, particularly the area near the dam, offers world-class bass and crappie fishing.

The ledge fishing for largemouth bass during summer is legendary, with anglers targeting schools of bass on deep-water ledges and humps. Spring crappie fishing draws enormous crowds to the lake's many bridge structures and stakebeds. The Land Between the Lakes National Recreation Area borders the lake's eastern shore, providing additional access points.

name: Lake Barkley
description:
Lake Barkley parallels Kentucky Lake on the Cumberland River, separated by the narrow Land Between the Lakes peninsula. A canal connects the two lakes, effectively creating one of the largest fishing complexes in the world.

Crappie fishing is the primary draw, with spring producing incredible catches around brush piles and bridge pilings. Largemouth bass fishing is excellent in the many coves and creek arms. The lake's proximity to Kentucky Lake allows anglers to fish both lakes in a single trip. Multiple state and Corps of Engineers ramps provide access.

name: Lake Cumberland
description:
Lake Cumberland is Kentucky's most popular lake, a deep, clear reservoir on the Cumberland River in south-central Kentucky. The lake is nationally renowned for its outstanding striped bass fishery.

Striped bass regularly exceed 30 pounds, with the lake producing fish over 50 pounds. The cold, deep water below Wolf Creek Dam also supports an excellent tailwater trout fishery. Smallmouth bass thrive along the rocky bluffs, and walleye provide action in the deeper sections. Nine marinas and numerous public ramps provide extensive access.

name: Dale Hollow Lake (KY)
description:
Dale Hollow Lake straddles the Kentucky-Tennessee border and holds the world record for smallmouth bass — a 11-pound, 15-ounce fish caught in 1955. The lake's exceptionally clear water and rocky habitat create ideal smallmouth conditions.

Smallmouth bass fishing remains world-class, with the lake consistently producing fish over 5 pounds. The clear water demands finesse techniques and light line. Walleye and musky add trophy potential, while crappie provide reliable panfish action. The Kentucky side offers several well-maintained access points.

name: Green River Lake
description:
Green River Lake is a Corps of Engineers reservoir in south-central Kentucky with clear water and excellent fishing for multiple species. The lake's deep, clear water and rocky structure support a diverse fishery.

Largemouth bass fishing is consistently productive in the creek arms and coves, while smallmouth bass inhabit the rocky main lake points. Musky have been stocked and are producing fish over 40 inches. The Green River below the dam is one of Kentucky's finest musky streams. Several public ramps and a marina provide access.

name: Barren River Lake
description:
Barren River Lake is a scenic reservoir in south-central Kentucky known for its excellent crappie and bass fishing. The lake's mix of clear and stained water zones creates diverse fishing opportunities.

Spring crappie fishing is the lake's calling card, with fish stacking up around bridge pilings and standing timber. Largemouth bass exceeding 8 pounds are caught annually, and the lake hosts several regional bass tournaments. White bass runs in the river arms provide fast spring action. The state resort park and public ramps offer convenient access.

name: Cave Run Lake
description:
Cave Run Lake in the Daniel Boone National Forest is Kentucky's premier musky fishery, producing trophy muskellunge that rival those of traditional musky waters in Wisconsin and Minnesota. The forested shoreline and clear water make it one of Kentucky's most scenic lakes.

Musky exceeding 50 inches are caught each year, with the lake record over 47 pounds. The diverse habitat also supports excellent bass fishing and quality crappie fishing in the many coves and creek arms. The remote location in eastern Kentucky's foothills keeps pressure moderate. Multiple Forest Service and state ramps provide access.

name: Nolin River Lake
description:
Nolin River Lake is a winding Corps of Engineers reservoir near Mammoth Cave National Park in west-central Kentucky. The lake's numerous arms and coves follow the Nolin River valley through scenic wooded hills.

Largemouth bass fishing is productive in the abundant cover and creek arms, with fish exceeding 7 pounds caught regularly. Crappie congregate around bridge pilings and brush piles during spring. The lake's proximity to Mammoth Cave makes it a popular destination for families combining fishing with cave tours. Multiple public ramps serve the lake.
